description: Multi-turn context-aware routing + conversation-focus regression guard. Reproduces (a) the keyword-less-follow-up misroute fixed by context-enriched, domain-coherent routing (docs/ROUTING-CONTEXT-FIX.md, bug-232) and (b) the multi-turn conversation-focus failures fixed by focal-entity + facet-carry + bias-to-fetch (bug-233): a name-only follow-up must not drag a prior subject back in and clarify, a pronoun/anaphor resolves to the last focal entity, a terse id-only follow-up inherits the prior turn's facet and answers, and a FOLLOW_UP needing not-yet-shown data falls through to a fetch. Drive as ONE conversation (LibreChat/BFF sends the growing window each turn). Principal rm_jane covers REL-00042 (Whitman) and REL-00099 (Calderon); Okafor is NOT in the book. No domain literal is embedded in the gateway — expected_domain is asserted from the winning agent manifest's own domain field.

turns.user: Give me a summary of the Whitman Family Office holdings
turns.note: Baseline in-coverage wealth query — must keep working.
Out-of-coverage: routes to wealth (has domain vocabulary), coverage CHECK denies. Clean deny, NOT misrouted to insurance.

turns.user: can you then summarize for Calderon Trust (REL-00099)?
turns.note: THE fix. Keyword-less follow-up; bare-message routing scored insurance.claim_status @0.312 and pruned wealth → wrong deny. Context-enriched routing now routes to wealth; Calderon is in the book → succeeds.

FOCAL ENTITY (bug-233): a terse ask that EXPLICITLY names the entity (name + id) is not ambiguous — it answers, it does not clarify. Supersedes the earlier bug-232 CLARIFY expectation: an explicitly-stated entity in the latest message wins.

FOCAL ENTITY (bug-233): a bare but explicitly-named entity ('Calderon Trust') is the subject → FETCH_DATA and answer (Calderon is in the book). Only a message that names NO entity and back-references none may clarify.

turns.user: now pull up the Whitman Family Office holdings
turns.note: FOCAL ENTITY (bug-233): the entity NAMED in the latest turn (Whitman) supersedes the entity discussed just before (Calderon) — the extractor must not drag the prior subject back in and manufacture a Whitman-vs-Calderon clarify. Whitman holdings answer.

turns.user: what's the performance on that portfolio?
turns.note: ANAPHORA + BIAS-TO-FETCH (bug-233): 'that portfolio' resolves to the last focal entity (Whitman); performance was not shown before, so the FOLLOW_UP must fall through to a fetch (bias-to-fetch) rather than answer 'no info' from memory.
Establishes a settlement facet on Whitman by explicit id; sets up the terse id-only follow-up below.

FACET CARRY + ID BACKSTOP (bug-233): the bare 'and for REL-00099?' inherits the prior turn's settlement facet (routing does not abstain on the margin because the turn carries an explicit grounded id) and the deterministic id_pattern backstop recovers REL-00099 when the LLM drops it. Calderon settlement answers.

turns.user: what's the latest NAV on fund FND-7781?
turns.note: Establishes a NAV/corporate-actions facet by explicit FUND id. FND- (fund_id) is a resolvable type but resource_scoped:false (corporate-actions), so it never coverage-grounds — sets up the terse non-coverage-scoped id follow-up below.

THE FND- analogue of mt_09 (routing spec V2.1 #4). The bare 'and FND-7782?' inherits the prior NAV facet. FND- is resolvable but resource_scoped:false, so it can NEVER RESOLVED_ALLOWED-ground; instead a deterministic manifest id_pattern match on the fund id earns the routing relaxation (V2.1 #4), so routing does not margin-abstain into a clarify. NAV/corporate-actions answers. Operator note: if FND-7782 is not seeded in the demo's fund inventory, swap in any second seeded FND- id — the row guards ROUTING (relaxation, no clarify), not that particular fund's data.
